general_info.definition: Fluorescent carbon nanoparticles used to modify TiO2 for enhanced SERS and photocatalysis

applications.performance: CDs were incorporated into yolk-shell SiO2@TiO2/CDs (y-STCs) nanocomposite. As a SERS substrate, the composite achieved detection limit for Rhodamine 6G (R6G) of 6×10-8 M. Good reproducibility with RSD <12% for Raman intensity at 613 cm-1. CDs extend photo-response to visible range (absorption peaks at 350 nm and 650 nm).
applications.comparative_advantage: CDs modification enhances SERS signals. Provides economical, noble-metal-free SERS substrate with high sensitivity and reproducibility.

applications.performance: y-STCs nanocomposite used for degradation of 2,4-dinitrophenol (DNP). Degradation rate reached 96% under simulated sunlight irradiation within 120 min. Excellent cycle stability: ~82% degradation after 5 cycles. CDs act as electron donors under light irradiation.
applications.comparative_advantage: CDs extend the absorption range of TiO2 to visible light, enhancing photocatalytic efficiency. Yolk-shell structure with CDs provides larger surface area and better contact with pollutants.
